codeReview.summary: The claimed fix feature has no worker session, handoff, or code diff of its own, and the original degradation commit still leaves the writer-visible persistence bug unfixed. The degradation path inserts a summary-note action, but it clears `review_runs.error_code`/`error_detail`, so the writer cannot render the explicit partial-coverage summary it is coded to use.

issues.description: The degradation short-circuit still updates the run with empty `ErrorCode` and `ErrorDetail` before returning. `writer.renderSummaryBody` only emits the persisted degradation summary when `run.ErrorCode == "degradation_mode"` and `run.ErrorDetail` contains the summary text, so the skipped-file explanation is not stored where the writer reads it and degraded summary rendering still falls back to the generic counts-only body. This means the original failure from `.factory/validation/beta-features/scrutiny/reviews/beta-large-mr-degradation.json` is not actually addressed.

The added regression only asserts that one `summary_note` action is queued; it never verifies that the run persisted `error_code`/`error_detail` for writer consumption or that the rendered note body includes the skipped files and reasons. Because the test omits the writer-visible persistence contract called for by the fix feature, it would pass even while the original bug remains.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `go-integration` skill says to capture the exact external contract and add a higher-level integration test proving the feature from its public entrypoint, but there is no worker session or handoff for this fix feature and the available regression coverage only checks comment-action insertion instead of the writer-visible persistence path that the contract depends on.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json` shows `beta-degradation-summary-persistence-fix.completedWorkerSessionId` is null, there is no matching handoff in `/Users/chris/.factory/missions/e68ebab7-a1ca-41e1-9c85-d3b7d9b36400/handoffs`, and `internal/llm/provider_test.go:280` only checks `ListCommentActionsByRun` for a `summary_note` row.

summary: Review failed. There is no distinct fix implementation to review, and the original degradation commit still clears the run fields that `writer.renderSummaryBody` requires for degraded partial-coverage output, so the skipped-file summary is not persisted end-to-end and the prior failure remains unresolved.
